clips.title: 飞机上的灵感变千万合同
clips.info_takeaway: 一位金融从业者用飞行途中灵感开发AI应用，迅速获得银行客户合同并融资。

source.episode: Replit's CEO on Vibe Coding, Wealth Building, and What Most People Get Wrong About AI

lines.en: One of my very recent startups that came out of Repl is a finance guy
lines.zh: 我最近一个从 Repl 出来的创业项目是个金融圈的人

lines.en: He was on a plane, and next to him was sitting an investment banker,
lines.zh: 他在飞机上，旁边坐着个投行的人

lines.en: just spending a lot of time building spreadsheets and building decks for clients.
lines.zh: 一直在做各种表格和给客户准备演示文稿

lines.en: He had an idea to automate a big part of that.
lines.zh: 他想到要把这事大部分自动化

lines.en: And he told him, I have an app for you.
lines.zh: 他跟那人说，我有个应用给你用

lines.en: And he didn't have an app.
lines.zh: 其实他根本没这个应用

lines.en: Can I come pitch it to you tomorrow?
lines.zh: 我明天能来给你介绍一下吗？

lines.en: He said, yeah, I mean, if you can solve this problem,
lines.zh: 那人说，可以啊，如果你能解决这个问题

lines.en: if you can make it faster for us to get to our clients.
lines.zh: 让我们更快地把东西交到客户手上

lines.en: He went home, and he's been using Repl.it just personally,
lines.zh: 他回家后就自己用 Repl.it

lines.en: just for fun, websites, things like that.
lines.zh: 就是玩玩，做些网站之类的。

lines.en: He spent the night working on the app.
lines.zh: 他一晚上都在搞那个应用。

lines.en: Next day, went and pitched it, left there with half a million dollars
lines.zh: 第二天去推介，出来就拿到五十万美元

lines.en: of letter of intent, and did this a few more times with other bankers.
lines.zh: 的意向书，后来又跟其他银行家重复了几次。

lines.en: And he's just raising at a $35 million valuation right now.
lines.zh: 他现在正按三千五百万的估值融资。

questions.question: What's the main idea of this story?
options: A. A finance guy quickly built an app to solve bankers' problems and turned it into a successful startup.
B. A banker taught someone how to make better spreadsheets.
C. Repl.it is only used for personal projects like websites.
D. Investment bankers often travel by plane for work.
questions.explanation_zh: 故事核心是一个金融人士抓住机会，用Repl.it快速开发应用解决银行家的需求，并取得巨大成功。

questions.question: How does the speaker seem to feel about this story?
options: A. Impressed by how quickly an idea turned into a valuable business.
B. Concerned that the app might not work well.
C. Neutral and just reporting facts.
D. Critical of the finance guy for making promises without an app.
questions.explanation_zh: 讲话者对这个故事持赞赏态度，强调从想法到高估值创业的快速转变。

clips.title: 教师用AI打造教育独角兽
clips.info_takeaway: 一位教师在疫情期间用AI开发教育工具，快速成长为年收入千万美元的公司。

source.episode: Replit's CEO on Vibe Coding, Wealth Building, and What Most People Get Wrong About AI

lines.en: We have an educator that's more of story from two years ago
lines.zh: 我们有个教育工作者的故事，大概是两年前的事

lines.en: because the company is pretty big right now,
lines.zh: 因为这家公司现在挺大了

lines.en: half a billion dollars worth.
lines.zh: 价值大概五亿美元

lines.en: But he's a teacher during COVID.
lines.zh: 但他在疫情期间还是个老师

lines.en: I think he just left his school and started playing around with AI,
lines.zh: 我觉得他就是离开学校开始玩 AI

lines.en: playing around with Repl.it,
lines.zh: 玩 Repl.it

lines.en: going in and trying his hand at coding with AI.
lines.zh: 进去试着用 AI 写代码

lines.en: And because he knows the problem space deeply,
lines.zh: 因为他对这个领域很熟

lines.en: he was able to build a lot of tools for teachers,
lines.zh: 他给老师做了很多工具

lines.en: for grading students, for creating assignments with AI.
lines.zh: 用 AI 帮忙批改学生作业、出作业

lines.en: And education is one of the hardest markets,
lines.zh: 教育是最难啃的市场之一

lines.en: but AI has this amazing ability to sell itself.
lines.zh: 但 AI 有种自带推销的本事

lines.en: And it quickly grew the company to $10 million annual revenue,
lines.zh: 它很快就把公司做到年收入一千万美元

lines.en: and now it's like half a billion dollars worth company.
lines.zh: 现在公司价值差不多五亿美元

questions.question: What's the main point of the story?
options: A. How a teacher used AI to create tools for education and grew a successful company
B. How schools struggled during COVID and needed more funding
C. How AI replaced teachers in classrooms
D. How coding is harder than teaching
questions.explanation_zh: 故事核心是讲一位教师利用AI开发教育工具并成功创办公司。

questions.question: What does the speaker seem to think about AI in education?
options: A. It's powerful and can quickly gain traction even in tough markets
B. It's too risky and not worth pursuing
C. It will only work in small niche areas
D. It can't help teachers much
questions.explanation_zh: 讲话者认为AI在教育这种难市场中也能迅速推广，显示其强大能力。

clips.title: AI十分钟生成品牌设计
clips.info_takeaway: 创业者用AI快速生成品牌设计包，解决品牌建设难题并低成本试错。

source.episode: Replit's CEO on Vibe Coding, Wealth Building, and What Most People Get Wrong About AI

lines.en: The other day I was on Twitter,
lines.zh: 前几天我在推特上

lines.en: I saw this guy who created an app quickly with Repl.it
lines.zh: 看到一个人用 Repl.it 很快做了个应用

lines.en: to generate brand kits and brand design material,
lines.zh: 用来生成品牌套件和设计素材

lines.en: logos, all of that.
lines.zh: 包括 logo 什么的

lines.en: You enter your product name, you go through a simple flow.
lines.zh: 你输入产品名字，按个简单流程走一遍

lines.en: It's called AnyMark.co.
lines.zh: 它叫 AnyMark.co

lines.en: And you pay, I think, $40 or something like that,
lines.zh: 你付大概 40 美元左右

lines.en: and you get an entire brand kit generated with AI.
lines.zh: 就能用 AI 生成一整套品牌套件

lines.en: So I see these stories every day.
lines.zh: 我每天都能看到这种故事

lines.en: And it's typically someone who has some domain knowledge
lines.zh: 通常是有点行业知识的人

lines.en: and a certain thing.
lines.zh: 还有某个东西。

lines.en: They realize there's a problem around them.
lines.zh: 他们意识到周围有问题。

lines.en: I see all my friends trying to start companies,
lines.zh: 我看到我的朋友们都在创业，

lines.en: but brands are really hard to build,
lines.zh: 但品牌真的很难做起来，

lines.en: so they automate that.
lines.zh: 所以他们就把它自动化了。

lines.en: So just look around you in the world
lines.zh: 所以你就看看周围的世界

lines.en: and just see what are the problems
lines.zh: 看看有哪些问题

lines.en: that people are dealing with
lines.zh: 是人们正在面对的

lines.en: that they're willing to pay for it.
lines.zh: 而且他们愿意为此付钱。

lines.en: And so to try things
lines.zh: 所以去尝试一下

questions.question: What's the main point of the speaker's story?
options: A. Using AI to quickly solve real-world problems can lead to useful products
B. Twitter is the best place to find new apps
C. Building a brand is easy if you have enough money
D. Repl.it is the only tool for creating apps
questions.explanation_zh: 讲者强调利用AI快速解决现实问题可以创造有用的产品，而不是专注于具体的平台或工具。

questions.question: How does the speaker feel about finding problems to solve?
options: A. It's a waste of time unless you already have a company
B. It's important to notice everyday challenges people will pay to fix
C. Only experts can identify valuable problems
D. Problems are too hard to solve without big teams
questions.explanation_zh: 讲者鼓励观察日常生活中的挑战，并寻找人们愿意付费解决的问题。

clips.title: 旅行公司如何守住护城河
clips.info_takeaway: Navon旅行公司依靠全球关系网和独特销售渠道，在AI冲击下仍能保持竞争力。

source.episode: Ben Horowitz on AI Infrastructure, Economics and The New Laws of Software

lines.en: this Navon right
lines.zh: 就是这个Navon对吧

lines.en: They're travel.
lines.zh: 他们是做旅游的

lines.en: So obviously, the SaaSpocalypse,
lines.zh: 所以显然是SaaSpocalypse

lines.en: like they're dead.
lines.zh: 就像他们已经完了

lines.en: No way you're doing travel.
lines.zh: 你根本不可能做旅游

lines.en: But then you look under the
lines.zh: 但你再深入看看

lines.en: covers and you go, well, it actually is a little more complicated than that
lines.zh: 你会发现其实没那么简单

lines.en: because on travel, you actually need explicit relationships.
lines.zh: 因为做旅游其实需要明确的关系

lines.en: If I'm providing
lines.zh: 如果我是提供

lines.en: your travel and you're any kind of company that's important at all, you need
lines.zh: 你的旅游，而且你是个有点重要的公司，你就需要

lines.en: to travel globally.
lines.zh: 去全球旅行。

lines.en: So now I need a relationship with every single airline
lines.zh: 所以我得跟每家航空公司都建立关系

lines.en: in the world, every single hotel in the world, every train, everything.
lines.zh: 全世界的酒店、火车，所有的一切。

lines.en: You've got
lines.zh: 你得

lines.en: to deal with that.
lines.zh: 处理这些事。

lines.en: You've got to kind of connect back to their budgeting systems
lines.zh: 还得接入他们的预算系统

lines.en: and all these things.
lines.zh: 以及各种东西。

lines.en: And then the second thing that's like nobody wants to do,
lines.zh: 还有第二件没人愿意干的事

lines.en: including OpenAI or Anthropic, is sell to the damn travel manager.
lines.zh: 包括 OpenAI 和 Anthropic，就是去卖给那个该死的差旅经理。

lines.en: Like nobody
lines.zh: 就没人

lines.en: has a channel to the travel manager.
lines.zh: 有个渠道能直接找旅行经理。

lines.en: And you can't even
lines.zh: 你甚至都不能

lines.en: imagine that being a good idea.
lines.zh: 想象这是个好主意。

lines.en: You want to keep advancing.
lines.zh: 你想继续往前走。

lines.en: You want to kind of do
lines.zh: 你想做点

lines.en: the things that Intuit is doing where like, OK, like turn ourselves into more
lines.zh: 像 Intuit 那样，把自己变得更

lines.en: of an AI company and then kind of hold the customer.
lines.zh: 像一家 AI 公司，然后抓住客户。

lines.en: And by the way, like the AI,
lines.zh: 顺便说一下，AI

lines.en: like the agentic travel experience turns out to be much more complicated than one
lines.zh: 那种智能旅行体验其实比想象中复杂得多

lines.en: would think.
lines.zh: 比你以为的要复杂。

lines.en: And I don't know if it that way but that's the way it is today
lines.zh: 我也不确定是不是这样，但今天就是这样

questions.question: What's the main point the speaker is making about AI in travel?
options: A. AI-powered travel is more complex than it seems due to relationships and systems
B. AI can easily replace all traditional travel services
C. Travel managers are eager to adopt AI solutions
D. The travel industry is already fully automated with AI
questions.explanation_zh: 讲话者强调，AI在旅行领域的实现比想象中复杂，需要大量关系和系统整合。

questions.question: How does the speaker feel about selling AI travel solutions to travel managers?
options: A. It's an exciting opportunity with huge potential
B. It's a bad idea that nobody wants to pursue
C. It's the easiest way to expand AI in travel
D. It's a necessary step for quick success
questions.explanation_zh: 讲话者认为向旅行经理销售AI旅行方案是没人想做的事，暗示这是个糟糕的想法。
